req.title: Hr-Admin deletes payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a payroll-run through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an hr-admin can delete a payroll-run via DELETE /api/payroll-runs, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and a 200/201 response on success. Error paths cover 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (wrong role). Tests are designed using EP (happy-path deletion), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role), BVA (minimal valid payload boundary), STC (state transition from existing to deleted), and DT (decision table covering auth/role matrix). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up payroll-run resources.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_payroll_run_success
preconditions: hr-admin is authenticated with a valid JWT
a payroll-run record exists and its ID is available
actions: Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture> and capture its entity_id
Assert response body contains the deleted entity_id
expected: Response body contains entity_id of deleted payroll-run
Audit record is written for the delete action
trace: R-WEB-039: authenticated hr-admin can delete payroll-run
R-WEB-039: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-039: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_payroll_run_success(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_payroll_run,
    assert_audit_record,
    api_base_url,
):
    entity_id = create_payroll_run(token=hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(entity_id) in str(body), (
        f"entity_id {entity_id} not found in response: {body}"
    )
    assert_audit_record(
        entity_id=entity_id,
        action="delete",
        actor_role="hr-admin",
    )

tests.name: test_delete_payroll_run_returns_401_when_unauthenticated
actions: Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture> using admin credentials
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{entity_id} WITHOUT any Authorization header
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_payroll_run_returns_401_when_unauthenticated(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_payroll_run,
    api_base_url,
):
    entity_id = create_payroll_run(token=hr_admin_token)

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}",
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )

tests.name: test_delete_payroll_run_returns_403_for_non_admin_role
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Obtain hr-admin token via <get_hr_admin_token> to create the resource
Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{entity_id} with employee Authorization header
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_payroll_run_returns_403_for_non_admin_role(
    hr_admin_token,
    employee_token,
    create_payroll_run,
    api_base_url,
):
    entity_id = create_payroll_run(token=hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )

tests.name: test_delete_payroll_run_returns_400_when_mandatory_field_missing
preconditions: hr-admin is authenticated with a valid JWT
DELETE request is sent with a blank/empty entity_id
actions: Send DELETE /api/payroll-runs/ with an empty entity_id segment
expected: Response body indicates mandatory field validation failure
trace: R-WEB-039: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_delete_payroll_run_state_transition_from_active_to_deleted
preconditions: hr-admin is authenticated with a valid JWT
actions: Create a payroll-run in ACTIVE state via <create_payroll_run_fixture>
Confirm the payroll-run is ACTIVE via GET /api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}
Attempt GET /api/payroll-runs/{entity_id} and assert it returns 404 or status DELETED
expected: Pre-delete GET returns HTTP 200 with status ACTIVE
DELETE returns HTTP 200 or 201 with entity_id
Post-delete GET returns 404 or body indicates DELETED state
Audit record written for the transition
trace: R-WEB-039: audit record written on success
R-WEB-039: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_payroll_run_state_transition_from_active_to_deleted(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_payroll_run,
    assert_audit_record,
    api_base_url,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    entity_id = create_payroll_run(token=hr_admin_token, status="ACTIVE")

    pre = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert pre.status_code == 200, f"Pre-delete GET failed: {pre.text}"

    del_resp = requests.delete(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"DELETE failed: {del_resp.status_code}: {del_resp.text}"
    )
    assert str(entity_id) in str(del_resp.json())

    post = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/payroll-runs/{entity_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert post.status_code in (404,) or \
        post.json().get("status") in ("DELETED", "deleted"), (
        f"Post-delete state unexpected: {post.status_code} {post.text}"
    )
    assert_audit_record(
        entity_id=entity_id, action="delete", actor_role="hr-admin"
    )

tests.name: test_delete_payroll_run_idempotency_on_already_deleted_resource
preconditions: hr-admin is authenticated with a valid JWT
payroll-run has already been deleted (boundary: entity at deletion edge)
actions: Create a payroll-run via <create_payroll_run_fixture> and capture entity_id
Send second DELETE /api/payroll-runs/{entity_id} against the now-absent resource
expected: No 5xx server error on repeated deletion
trace: R-WEB-039: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-039: 400 on validation error (entity no longer valid target)
